description: Understand what Git is, why it exists, and how it helps developers work safely and collaboratively.
Learn what version control is, why it exists, and how it helps you work safely.
Learn the essential terminal commands you'll use throughout this Git workshop.
Install Git and configure your identity so your work is properly tracked.
Create your first Git repository and understand the basic Git workflow.
Learn the core Git workflow used in everyday development.
Understand what GitHub is, why developers use it, and how it works with Git.

lessonTitle: Pushing a Local Repository to GitHub
description: Learn how to connect a local Git repository to GitHub and upload your code.
Learn how to download a GitHub repository to your computer and keep it up to date.

lessonTitle: Using HTTPS vs SSH with GitHub
description: Learn the difference between HTTPS and SSH, and set up SSH authentication with GitHub.

lessonTitle: Building Our First Project with Git
description: Create a simple guessing game and make your first meaningful project commit.
Learn how projects evolve through small, meaningful commits.
Learn the simple workflow that many developers follow every day when working on their own projects.

lessonTitle: Homework: Build a CLI Todo App with Git
description: Practice Git by building a Todo application through a series of meaningful commits.
